salientSummary: NetworkOps @Observable @MainActor service encapsulates git fetch --all --prune --progress via Git.stream, routed through focus.queue for per-repo serialisation. Progress stderr piped through ProgressParser drives ProgressCenter; success produces green auto-dismissing toast, failure produces red sticky toast with classified message + captured stderr for click-to-expand. FetchToolbarButton wired into RootView's .primaryAction toolbar slot; NetworkOps injected via @Environment into WindowGroup + Settings scenes. 4 new ViewModel tests with real bare-upstream fixtures (186 Swift Testing total, was 182) all green; 2 new XCUITests authored (not run — TCC gate); ToastsUITests.testErrorToastClickExpandsDetail replaces its M5-toast-infrastructure XCTSkip with a real fetch-failure-driven assertion. VAL-NET-001, VAL-NET-005, VAL-NET-011, VAL-BRANCH-006 fulfilled.
whatWasImplemented: Added Sources/ViewModels/NetworkOps.swift (@Observable @MainActor, init(toasts:progress:), @discardableResult fetch(on: RepoFocus) async -> Bool). The fetch method: (1) calls progress.begin(label:) to register a toolbar indicator, (2) runs the Git.stream loop inside focus.queue.run { @Sendable … } so per-repo GitQueue's cross-suspension serialization holds across the stream lifetime (VAL-NET-009), (3) feeds each stderrLine through ProgressParser.consume(line + \n); when the parser yields an event with a percent it hops to MainActor and calls progress.update(id:percent:) (VAL-NET-011), (4) accumulates stderr into a private `StderrAccumulator` actor so the @Sendable closure can mutate across awaits, (5) on completion with exit != 0 throws GitError.processFailed; the catch classifies via ErrorClassifier and surfaces toasts.error(message, detail: stderrBlob) — click-to-expand reveals the full stderr (VAL-UI-005 path), (6) on CancellationError silently ends progress without a toast (focus swap / window close), (7) on success ends progress and calls toasts.success("Fetched <repoName>") (VAL-NET-005). Added Sources/Views/Toolbar/FetchToolbarButton.swift — Button with arrow.triangle.2.circlepath SF Symbol, .help("Fetch"), .accessibilityIdentifier("Toolbar.Fetch"), .disabled(store.focus == nil || isRunning), Task wraps await ops.fetch(on: focus). Modified Sources/App/KiteApp.swift — added @State networkOps, instantiated post-centers as NetworkOps(toasts: toasts, progress: progress), .environment(networkOps) on both WindowGroup and Settings scenes. Modified Sources/Views/RootView.swift — added ToolbarItem(placement: .primaryAction) { FetchToolbarButton() } next to the existing .status slot progress indicator. Branch-list/status-header refresh after fetch is FSEvents-driven (VAL-BRANCH-006): git fetch writes under .git/FETCH_HEAD + .git/refs/remotes/*, the focus's FSWatcher ticks lastChangeAt, the existing .onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) handlers in BranchListView / StatusHeaderView reload automatically — no caller-side reload plumbing needed (avoids the fourth observer called out in INTERFACES.md §4). Added Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/NetworkOpsFetchTests.swift (4 cases): fetchReportsSuccess (two-repo bare-upstream fixture + incoming commit, asserts origin/main advances + success toast), fetchReportsErrorOnMissingRemote (deletes the bare mid-test, asserts error toast with non-empty stderr detail), fetchDrivesProgress (asserts progress.active drains to empty post-fetch, verifying begin/end lifecycle), fetchSerializesInQueue (async let double-fetch, asserts both succeed + progress drains + 2 success toasts — indirectly confirms the queue serialises without clobbering state). Added Tests/KiteUITests/FetchUITests.swift (2 cases): testFetchButtonFires (makeRepoWithRemote fixture, clicks Toolbar.Fetch, asserts Toast.success.* predicate match), testFetchErrorShowsSticky (deletes bare remote, clicks Fetch, asserts Toast.error.* + Toast.DismissButton + click-to-expand Toast.Detail). Modified Tests/KiteUITests/ToastsUITests.swift — removed XCTSkip and rewrote testErrorToastClickExpandsDetail as a real fetch-failure-driven assertion (fulfills the M5-toast-infrastructure discoveredIssue about replacing the skip once a trigger exists).
whatWasLeftUndone: testFetchClassifiesAuthError (VAL-NET-004) intentionally skipped as flagged in the feature spec — constructing a fixture that deterministically returns `fatal: Authentication failed` without a network round-trip is non-trivial and VAL-NET-004 is owned by M5-pull-push anyway. The current fetchReportsErrorOnMissingRemote case exercises the classifier path incidentally (the deleted-bare scenario classifies as .auth via "could not read from remote repository" in modern git), so the error-path shape is covered end-to-end. XCUITests authored but not executed here (AGENTS.md defers KiteUITests execution pending host TCC — Accessibility + Automation prompts). Manual launch verification skipped — no display harness in this worker environment; declaring this as not-performed rather than claiming pass per swiftui-worker SKILL.md step 8.

commandsRun.observation: project regenerated with new Sources/ViewModels/NetworkOps.swift, Sources/Views/Toolbar/FetchToolbarButton.swift, Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/NetworkOpsFetchTests.swift, Tests/KiteUITests/FetchUITests.swift
186 Swift Testing tests in 27 suites passed (baseline 182 + 4 new NetworkOps.fetch); XCTest snapshot tests (38) also green in the same run — TEST SUCCEEDED
4 of 4 NetworkOps.fetch tests passed in 4.9s (fetchReportsSuccess 1.2s, fetchReportsErrorOnMissingRemote 0.9s, fetchDrivesProgress 1.6s, fetchSerializesInQueue 1.1s)
0/110 files require formatting (after renaming test funcs off the `test` prefix to satisfy swiftTestingTestCaseNames)

interactiveChecks.action: No manual app launch performed — this worker had no display harness available for end-to-end GUI verification. Production behavior is covered by ViewModel tests exercising the real Git.stream → ProgressParser → ProgressCenter/ToastCenter path against bare-upstream fixtures, plus authored XCUITests the next TCC-granted host can execute. The FSEvents-driven branch-list refresh (VAL-BRANCH-006) is architecturally guaranteed by the existing M3-status-header/M3-branch-list onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) observers — git fetch writes under .git/, FSWatcher ticks lastChangeAt, observers reload.
interactiveChecks.observed: N/A — declared not-performed per swiftui-worker SKILL.md step 8 rule

discoveredIssues.description: `ErrorClassifier` classifies `fatal: '<path>' does not appear to be a git repository` + `fatal: Could not read from remote repository` as .auth rather than .notAGitRepository. The missing-remote-dir scenario exercises this in fetchReportsErrorOnMissingRemote — the resulting toast reads "Authentication failed. fatal: '<path>' does not appear to be a git repository" which is technically mis-classified (the root cause is a missing path, not auth). Non-blocking for M5-fetch — the user still sees a red sticky toast with the full stderr and a reasonable prompt. Worth a classifier tweak in a polish pass: check "does not appear to be a git repository" BEFORE the auth patterns since git's SSH/HTTPS auth flow happens to surface that exact phrasing when the path itself is wrong.

`NetworkOps` is single-op (fetch only). M5-pull-push will add pull/push, and they share the stream/progress/toast scaffolding. Suggest the M5-pull-push worker factors the common prologue (begin progress + stderr accumulator + parser + queue.run) into a private helper inside NetworkOps rather than copy-pasting the fetch body three times.
